title: Eliminate internal trade barriers and build a unified Canadian economy
description: A broad commitment to strengthen the domestic economy by removing interprovincial trade barriers, harmonizing regulations, and reducing federal red tape to offset the impacts of US tariffs.
original_text: To do this, there must be one Canadian economy, not thirteen. That’s why Mark Carney has a plan to remove barriers to internal trade. We will reduce internal trade costs by up to 15% and expand our economy by up to $200 billion

title: Table legislation to eliminate all federal barriers to interprovincial trade by Canada Day
description: Mechanism involves tabling federal legislation to eliminate interprovincial trade barriers and remove federal exceptions under the Canada Free Trade Agreement, targeting completion by July 1 to counter economic impacts of US tariffs.
original_text: Unleash free trade in Canada by Canada Day by tabling legislation to eliminate all federal barriers to interprovincial trade and labour mobility and to remove all federal exceptions under the Canada Free Trade Agreement.
parent_title: Eliminate internal trade barriers and build a unified Canadian economy

title: Achieve mutual recognition of professional and safety credentials with provinces and territories
description: Requires federal coordination with provinces and territories to establish mutual recognition or harmonization for professional credentials, specifically targeting healthcare, financial services, and trucking to improve interprovincial labour mobility.
original_text: Achieve mutual recognition of credentials with provinces and territories so Canadians can work wherever they want. This includes health professional credentials, professional qualifications in financial services, and trucking safety certifications, amongst others. In cases where mutual recognition is not attainable, we will harmonize regulations to enhance labour mobility.
parent_title: Eliminate internal trade barriers and build a unified Canadian economy

title: Require federal departments to report within 60 days on eliminating outdated rules and reducing red tape
description: Imposes a strict 60-day reporting deadline on federal departments to identify and eliminate outdated rules, reduce duplication with provincial regulations, and streamline regulatory administration.
original_text: Reduce red tape. We will require all relevant federal departments – such as Transport Canada, Natural Resources Canada, and Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada – to undertake reviews and to report on progress, within 60 days, on steps to eliminate outdated or unnecessary rules, reduce duplication or overlap with provincial rules, and streamline the administration of rules and the delivery of regulatory decisions.
parent_title: Eliminate internal trade barriers and build a unified Canadian economy

title: Invest in large-scale nation-building infrastructure projects across Canada
description: A comprehensive infrastructure strategy aimed at enhancing domestic supply chains, trade corridors, and community connectivity through large-scale capital investments.
original_text: It’s time to unite this country and invest in nation-building infrastructure on a scale not seen in generations. Major nation-building projects will connect Canada and grow the economy in ways that last for generations

title: Fund critical infrastructure projects including the George Massey Tunnel, Edmonton-Calgary rail, Québec City Tramway, and Grays Bay Port
description: Commits federal financial contributions and intergovernmental collaboration to advance specific major regional transit and transportation infrastructure projects across multiple provinces and territories.
original_text: Invest to build infrastructure that connects Canada, breaks down barriers, and brings people and economies closer together. We will work with all orders of government and Indigenous Peoples on critical infrastructure that connects communities. These could include contributions to projects such as the George Massey Tunnel that connects Richmond to Delta; supporting Alberta’s work on Edmonton to Calgary passenger rail; the Québec City Tramway; the Grays Bay Port and Road Project connecting the Arctic Ocean to Yellowknife and into southern Canada; and other projects such as the twinning of the Trans-Canada Highway.
parent_title: Invest in large-scale nation-building infrastructure projects across Canada

title: Increase affordability for the Confederation Bridge, Northumberland and Marine Atlantic, and CTMA Ferries
description: Targeted financial intervention to lower the cost of major tolled bridge and ferry connections serving Prince Edward Island and other Atlantic regions.
original_text: Make the Confederation Bridge, Northumberland and Marine Atlantic, and the Coopérative de Transport Maritime et Aérien Ferries more affordable. We will ensure fair and equal treatment for PEI’s bridge and ferry access.
parent_title: Invest in large-scale nation-building infrastructure projects across Canada

title: Build high-speed rail connecting Windsor to Québec City
description: Commits to the construction of a dedicated high-speed passenger rail corridor spanning the Windsor to Québec City corridor.
original_text: Build high-speed rail that connects communities between Windsor and Québec City.
parent_title: Invest in large-scale nation-building infrastructure projects across Canada

title: Establish a new $5 billion Trade Diversification Corridors Fund for trade-enabling infrastructure
description: Allocates a specific dollar amount ($5 billion) to a newly created fund dedicated to expanding ports, railways, airports, and highways to facilitate interprovincial and international trade.
original_text: Build new trade infrastructure that connects Canada through the new $5 billion Trade Diversification Corridors Fund, including ports, railways, airports, highways, and other trade enabling infrastructure.
parent_title: Invest in large-scale nation-building infrastructure projects across Canada

title: Increase the Indigenous Loan Guarantee Program to $10 billion and expand its sectoral scope
description: Explicitly doubles the capital allocation of the Indigenous Loan Guarantee Program to $10 billion and broadens the eligible sectors to ensure greater Indigenous equity in major resource and infrastructure projects.
original_text: Advance nation-building infrastructure projects through the recently doubled Indigenous Loan Guarantee Program from $5 billion to $10 billion and increasing the sectoral scope. This will make it easier for more Indigenous communities to have a greater share in the benefits of major projects
parent_title: Invest in large-scale nation-building infrastructure projects across Canada

title: Build a nation-building east-west electricity grid to enhance clean energy connectivity
description: Commits to developing an interprovincial electricity transmission network to distribute low-emissions power from large producers (like Hydro-Québec and Manitoba Hydro) to industrial and residential markets.
original_text: Build out Canada’s east-west electricity grid, a nation-building transmission project developed with provinces, territories, and Indigenous partners, and stakeholders. This commitment will unleash clean growth across the country, connecting more homes and businesses to the power of clean electricity
parent_title: Invest in large-scale nation-building infrastructure projects across Canada

title: Invest an additional $250 million in the Rural Transit Solutions Fund
description: Injects a specific additional monetary amount ($250 million) into an existing fund to subsidize local and regional transit operations in non-urban areas.
original_text: Make rural and small communities more affordable and accessible through investing an additional $250 million in the Rural Transit Solutions Fund.
parent_title: Invest in large-scale nation-building infrastructure projects across Canada

title: Streamline and accelerate federal project review and approval processes
description: A structural reform initiative designed to drastically reduce approval timelines for major resource and infrastructure projects through centralized federal oversight and inter-jurisdictional cooperation.
original_text: It’s time for the government to commit its powers, policy instruments, and fiscal capacity to speed up the review process so we can build major projects in the national interest.
source_section: Getting Projects Done Faster and Better

title: Establish a Major Federal Project Office to provide 'One Window' project decisions
description: Creates a new dedicated federal entity to serve as a single point of contact for businesses navigating federal project regulations, aiming to eliminate redundancies.
original_text: Get big projects built quickly by establishing “One Window” project decisions through a Major Federal Project Office and effectively shifting the focus of project review from “why” to “how.”
source_section: Getting Projects Done Faster and Better
parent_title: Streamline and accelerate federal project review and approval processes

title: Mandate a maximum two-year timeline for final decisions on major projects
description: Sets a strict 24-month statutory limit for the new Major Federal Project Office to issue final project approvals, significantly accelerating the previous five-year baseline to improve investment certainty.
original_text: Require the new Major Federal Project Office to render final decisions on projects on a maximum two-year timeline, much faster than the former government’s five-year timeline. This will include where other federal decisions are required, including under the Fisheries Act, the Species at Risk Act, and other federal legislation.
source_section: Getting Projects Done Faster and Better
parent_title: Streamline and accelerate federal project review and approval processes

title: Sign Cooperation and Substitution Agreements within six months for One Project, One Review
description: Imposes a six-month deadline to execute formal agreements allowing the federal government to recognize and substitute provincial, territorial, and Indigenous environmental assessments for federal ones.
original_text: Move forward with One Project, One Review. Within six months, we will sign Cooperation and Substitution Agreements with all willing Premiers, as well as Indigenous Governing Bodies, allowing the federal government to recognize provincial, territorial, and Indigenous-led assessments.
source_section: Getting Projects Done Faster and Better
parent_title: Streamline and accelerate federal project review and approval processes

title: Double capacity support funding for Indigenous communities to participate in project decisions
description: Increases funding by 100% to support the administrative and technical capacity of Indigenous communities to participate meaningfully in project assessment and decision-making processes.
original_text: Double capacity support so more Indigenous communities can take an active role in project decisions at various stages, and to improve meaningful and immediate participation
source_section: Getting Projects Done Faster and Better
parent_title: Streamline and accelerate federal project review and approval processes

title: Strengthen the mandate and funding of CBC/Radio-Canada and support Canadian creators
description: A suite of policies to insulate the public broadcaster from political funding changes, modernize its mandate, and boost financial support for Canadian cultural agencies.
original_text: In this time of crisis, protecting Canada means protecting our culture, our journalism, our perspectives... A Mark Carney-led government will: Enshrine and protect CBC/Radio Canada

title: Introduce legislation to establish statutory funding for CBC/Radio-Canada
description: Will introduce a bill in Parliament to establish a permanent, statutory funding model for the public broadcaster, removing it from the annual discretionary budget cycle.
original_text: Enshrine and protect CBC/Radio Canada by introducing legislation to establish statutory funding. Funding for our national public broadcaster should not be subject to the whims of government; our national institutions deserve to be protected by Parliament and changed only with the consent of the House of Commons and the people it represents.
parent_title: Strengthen the mandate and funding of CBC/Radio-Canada and support Canadian creators

title: Provide an initial $150 million boost in annual funding for CBC/Radio-Canada
description: Commits an immediate $150 million increase to the broadcaster's annual budget, with a long-term goal of matching the per-capita funding averages of international public broadcasters.
original_text: Provide an initial $150 million boost in annual funding while directing CBC/Radio Canada to develop a strategic plan consistent with this new mandate. We will work towards bringing CBC/Radio Canada’s long-term funding levels in line with the average funding of other national public broadcasters over time.
parent_title: Strengthen the mandate and funding of CBC/Radio-Canada and support Canadian creators

title: Increase funding for the Canada Council for the Arts, Telefilm, Canada Media Fund, and National Film Board
description: Promises budget increases to specific federal cultural agencies (Canada Council for the Arts, Telefilm, Canada Media Fund, National Film Board) to stimulate the domestic creative industry.
original_text: Support Canadian artists and creators by increasing funding to agencies such as the Canada Council for the Arts, Telefilm, the Canada Media Fund, and the National Film Board, recognizing the economic importance of Canada’s creative industries and creators.
parent_title: Strengthen the mandate and funding of CBC/Radio-Canada and support Canadian creators

title: Introduce a Canada Strong Pass offering free museum access and VIA Rail seats for youth under 18 from June to August 2025
description: Creates a temporary summer program (June-August 2025) subsidizing travel and cultural access for youth, including fully free access for under-18s and heavy discounts for 18-24 year olds.
original_text: Introduce a Canada Strong Pass for three months this summer. The Canada Strong Pass will provide children and youth under the age of 18 with free access to Canada’s incredible national galleries and museums, and free seats on VIA Rail when they travel with their parents. We will work with willing provinces and territories to establish similar pricing structures in our beautiful provincial museums and galleries. This pass will be available from June to August 2025. We will also introduce heavily discounted access and fares for young Canadians aged 18-24.

title: Provide free access to National Parks and Historic Sites and reduce camping fees for all Canadians during summer 2025
description: Waives admission fees for all federal parks and historic sites and subsidizes camping fees nationwide for the summer 2025 season.
original_text: Make access to National Parks and Historic Sites free this summer for everyone. We will also reduce prices for camping sites in national parks for all Canadians from June to August.

title: Implement a comprehensive Buy Canadian strategy across federal procurement and consumer markets
description: A comprehensive protectionist strategy leveraging federal purchasing power and regulatory changes to favor domestic suppliers across infrastructure, defence, and consumer goods in response to US trade tariffs.
original_text: It’s time for the Government of Canada to give Canadian companies due consideration in federal procurement. Buying Canadian gives companies the confidence to build, to hire, and to stand strong in the face of this crisis.

title: Increase stringency of product origin labelling and support Competition Bureau enforcement
description: Mandates stricter regulatory standards for "Made in Canada" labelling to improve consumer transparency, backed by financial transition support for manufacturers and enforcement by the Competition Bureau.
original_text: Make it easy for Canadians to Buy Canadian. Working with retailers, agricultural producers, manufacturers, food and beverage companies, and by maximizing Canada's supply chain we will increase transparency and increase stringency in origin of product labelling so that it is simple, clear, and easy to identify what is a truly Canadian product. To help make this happen, we will provide support to help manufacturers adapt and for the Competition Bureau to enforce these new requirements.
parent_title: Implement a comprehensive Buy Canadian strategy across federal procurement and consumer markets

title: Deploy a made-in-Canada procurement strategy prioritizing domestic suppliers and limiting foreign bidders
description: Alters federal procurement rules to explicitly favor domestic suppliers and restrict foreign bidders to the minimum required by existing free trade agreements, with specific directives for defence and Crown corporations.
original_text: Deploy a made-in-Canada procurement strategy that prioritizes, whenever possible, Canadian suppliers and supply chains, and limiting bidders from foreign suppliers to bidding only on what we have agreed to in Canada's Free Trade Agreements. We will prioritize Canadian contractors in our defence procurement, including the Canadian aerospace industry. Crown Corporations with major capital acquisitions, like VIA Rail, will also be encouraged to meet this standard.
parent_title: Implement a comprehensive Buy Canadian strategy across federal procurement and consumer markets

title: Establish Buy Canadian standards for federal infrastructure funding to maximize domestic steel, aluminum, and forestry products
description: Attaches domestic content requirements to federal infrastructure grants, specifically targeting the utilization of Canadian-produced steel, aluminum, and forestry materials.
original_text: Establish Buy Canadian standards for federal infrastructure funding including maximizing Canadian steel, aluminum, and forestry products.
parent_title: Implement a comprehensive Buy Canadian strategy across federal procurement and consumer markets

title: Exceed the NATO defence spending target of 2% of GDP before 2030
description: Commits to surpassing the NATO benchmark of spending 2% of GDP on defence prior to the year 2030, accelerating previous timelines to address geopolitical threats and sovereignty concerns.
original_text: Our investments will be spent wisely and effectively to put Canada on track to exceed our NATO defence spending target before 2030.

title: Rebuild and Support the Canadian Armed Forces Personnel
description: A comprehensive initiative to improve the working conditions, compensation, and support systems for members of the Canadian Armed Forces to boost recruitment and retention.
original_text: We will fix these shortcomings and invest to make the CAF one of the best workplaces in the country... A Mark Carney-led government will: Give a pay raise to CAF members... Give better support to our CAF members, through investments in housing, health care, and child care... Modernize our recruitment process... Address the external recommendations to combat military sexual misconduct.

title: Increase Canadian Armed Forces Personnel Pay
description: Increase compensation for active military personnel to improve retention and reflect the increasing demands of protecting Canadian sovereignty.
original_text: Give a pay raise to CAF members to recognize that we are asking more of them as Canada steps up to defend our sovereignty.
parent_title: Rebuild and Support the Canadian Armed Forces Personnel

title: Increase High-Quality Housing Stock on Military Bases
description: Accelerate the construction and availability of high-quality residential units on military bases nationwide to support serving members and their families.
original_text: This means rapidly increasing the stock of high-quality housing on bases across the country
parent_title: Rebuild and Support the Canadian Armed Forces Personnel

title: Ensure Access to Primary and Mental Health Care for Military Members
description: Provide guaranteed access to primary healthcare and mental health services specifically tailored for active military members and their families.
original_text: ensuring access to primary child care and health care – including mental health supports – for serving members and their families.
parent_title: Rebuild and Support the Canadian Armed Forces Personnel

title: Modernize the Military Recruitment and Security Clearance Process
description: Streamline the military application process by introducing online applications and expediting security clearances to address the sub-10% recruitment success rate.
original_text: Modernize our recruitment process, including by streamlining security clearances and applying online, so that more applicants can get trained, faster.
parent_title: Rebuild and Support the Canadian Armed Forces Personnel

title: Implement External Recommendations to Combat Military Sexual Misconduct
description: Implement the recommendations from external reviews regarding military sexual misconduct to ensure a safe workplace for all Canadian Armed Forces members.
original_text: Address the external recommendations to combat military sexual misconduct. Sexual misconduct has absolutely no place in the Canadian Armed Forces.
parent_title: Rebuild and Support the Canadian Armed Forces Personnel

title: Procure New Equipment to Rearm the Canadian Armed Forces
description: A broad commitment to procure advanced military equipment across the Army, Navy, Air Force, and Coast Guard to meet NATO targets and defend sovereignty.
original_text: We have a plan to meet our NATO commitments and ensure that the CAF has the equipment they need to defend our sovereignty. That means dozens of new investments in equipment for our military, including foundational investments.

title: Procure New Submarines and Heavy Icebreakers
description: Purchase new submarines and heavy icebreakers to enhance maritime domain awareness and secure Canada's Arctic coastlines.
original_text: Expand our ability to monitor our coastlines and secure Canada’s Arctic, including with new submarines for the Royal Canadian Navy and additional heavy icebreakers, building on our commitment to all orders underway through the National Shipbuilding Strategy.
parent_title: Procure New Equipment to Rearm the Canadian Armed Forces

title: Expand the Fleet of Aerial and Underwater Drones
description: Acquire a larger fleet of aerial and underwater drones to establish continuous surveillance capabilities in the Arctic and along national borders.
original_text: Expand our fleet of aerial and underwater drones to build a persistent presence in the air and sea, including persistent surveillance to protect against threats in the Arctic and at our border.
parent_title: Procure New Equipment to Rearm the Canadian Armed Forces

title: Procure Canadian-Made Airborne Early Warning and Control Aircraft
description: Procure domestically manufactured airborne early warning and control (AEW&C) aircraft to accelerate procurement and support the local aerospace sector.
original_text: Buy Canadian-made airborne early warning and control aircraft, supporting our domestic aerospace industry and accelerating the procurement process.
parent_title: Procure New Equipment to Rearm the Canadian Armed Forces

title: Invest in Self-Propelled Artillery and Ground-Based Air Defence Systems
description: Invest in modernizing the Canadian Army's capabilities by acquiring new self-propelled artillery systems and ground-based air defence.
original_text: Support the Canadian Army with significant new investments, such as new self-propelled artillery systems and ground-based air defence capabilities so that the CAF have the capacity to operate in today’s context.
parent_title: Procure New Equipment to Rearm the Canadian Armed Forces

title: Update the Canadian Coast Guard Mandate for Maritime Surveillance
description: Update the mandate of the Canadian Coast Guard to explicitly include sovereignty protection, maritime surveillance, and integration into NATO defence capabilities.
original_text: Give the Canadian Coast Guard a new mandate and the right equipment to conduct maritime surveillance operations to secure our coasts... Expanding the reach and abilities of the Canadian Coast Guard will also mean integrating them into Canada’s NATO defence capabilities.
parent_title: Procure New Equipment to Rearm the Canadian Armed Forces

title: Provide New Equipment for Military Reserves and Canadian Rangers
description: Provide modernized equipment to the Military Reserves and Canadian Rangers to support domestic disaster response and sovereignty protection.
original_text: Invest in our Reserves and Canadian Rangers to give them the equipment they need to continue protecting our sovereignty and conduct domestic disaster responses that directly helps Canadians.
parent_title: Procure New Equipment to Rearm the Canadian Armed Forces

title: Reinvest in Defence Industries and Reform Procurement
description: A structural overhaul of how Canada procures defence equipment, focusing on speed, domestic sourcing, and technological innovation.
original_text: We must also reform the way Canada procures for its defence so that we do not suffer undue delays in equipment deliveries... Overhaul defence procurement to better meet our needs and the urgency of the moment

title: Establish the Bureau of Research, Engineering and Advanced Leadership in Science (BOREALIS)
description: Create a new research bureau (BOREALIS) to supply the CAF and Communications Security Establishment with domestic innovations in AI, quantum computing, and cybersecurity.
original_text: Establish the Bureau of Research, Engineering and Advanced Leadership in Science (BOREALIS) to ensure the Canadian Armed Forces and Communications Security Establishment have the made-in-Canada innovation solutions they need in areas such as AI, quantum computing, cybersecurity, and other advanced research and technology.
parent_title: Reinvest in Defence Industries and Reform Procurement

title: Establish a New Defence Procurement Agency
description: Create a centralized Defence Procurement Agency to streamline the military equipment acquisition process.
original_text: Establishing a new Defence Procurement Agency.
parent_title: Reinvest in Defence Industries and Reform Procurement

title: Amend Legislation to Streamline Defence Procurement Approvals
description: Modify procurement laws and regulations to introduce risk-based approval processes and centralize government expertise for faster military acquisitions.
original_text: Amending legislation and regulations as required, including taking steps to expand risk-based approaches to approvals, to centralize expertise from across government and streamline the way we buy equipment for the military.
parent_title: Reinvest in Defence Industries and Reform Procurement

title: Prioritize Canadian Sourcing and Raw Materials in Defence Procurement
description: Implement procurement policies that prioritize domestic sourcing of finished goods and raw materials like steel, aluminum, and critical minerals.
original_text: Buying Canadian whenever possible and prioritizing Canadian raw materials such as steel, aluminum and critical minerals.
parent_title: Reinvest in Defence Industries and Reform Procurement

title: Advance Canadian Involvement in the ReArm Europe Plan
description: Integrate Canada's domestic defence industry into the ReArm Europe Plan to support transatlantic security and drive domestic investment.
original_text: Boosting Canada’s domestic defence industries and production... That includes advancing Canada’s involvement in the ReArm Europe Plan in support of transatlantic security, and prioritizing investment here in Canada
parent_title: Reinvest in Defence Industries and Reform Procurement

title: Strengthen Arctic Sovereignty and Northern Infrastructure
description: A comprehensive strategy to enhance Arctic security, infrastructure, and Indigenous partnerships to protect sovereignty and support Northern communities.
original_text: With our sovereignty under threat, it is time to work with Indigenous and Northern partners to strengthen the North, to deter incursions, and to strengthen our presence in the Arctic... A Mark Carney-led government will:

title: Partner with Indigenous Leadership on Dual-Use Arctic Infrastructure
description: Collaborate with Arctic and Northern Indigenous leaders to ensure defence investments respect traditional knowledge and focus on dual-use infrastructure like airstrips.
original_text: Work closely with Arctic and Northern Indigenous leadership as partners, in defence and security investments that respect their rights, incorporate traditional knowledge, and ensure community priorities are reflected, especially on targeted investments in dual-use infrastructure such as airstrips and other critical infrastructure.
parent_title: Strengthen Arctic Sovereignty and Northern Infrastructure

title: Develop a Network of Arctic Deepwater Ports
description: Fund the construction of a network of deepwater ports in the Arctic to enable year-round protection of the Northwest Passage by the Canadian Armed Forces.
original_text: Strengthen the presence of Canadian Armed Forces in the Arctic to protect the sovereignty of our land, air, and sea, including through a network of deepwater ports that will allow us to protect the Northwest Passage year-round.
parent_title: Strengthen Arctic Sovereignty and Northern Infrastructure

title: Develop Advanced Over-the-Horizon Radar with Australia
description: Collaborate with Australia to develop advanced Over-the-Horizon Radar for early warning threat detection in the Arctic.
original_text: Move forward to detect and deter early warning radar coverage of threats to the Arctic by working with partners in Australia to develop advanced Over-the-Horizon Radar. This will also help deepen our defence and trade ties with Australia.
parent_title: Strengthen Arctic Sovereignty and Northern Infrastructure

title: Invest in Northern Hydroelectricity and Energy Projects
description: Fund a new hydroelectricity project and other energy initiatives in the North to provide safe and reliable energy to communities.
original_text: Invest in Northern infrastructure, including a new hydroelectricity project and other energy projects, so that people have access to safe, reliable energy
parent_title: Strengthen Arctic Sovereignty and Northern Infrastructure

title: Accelerate Northern Housing Development
description: Fund initiatives to improve existing housing stock and accelerate new housing development to meet growing demand in Northern communities.
original_text: improve housing stock, accelerate housing development, and meet growing housing demand.
parent_title: Strengthen Arctic Sovereignty and Northern Infrastructure

title: Invest in Dual-Use Northern Transportation Infrastructure
description: Fund the construction of new dual-use transportation infrastructure in the North, including ports, railways, airstrips, and highways, in partnership with Indigenous groups.
original_text: We will also invest in new port projects, railways, airstrips, and highways, all with dual-use capabilities. This work will be undertaken with Arctic and Northern Indigenous partners.
parent_title: Strengthen Arctic Sovereignty and Northern Infrastructure

title: Forge a Canada-Europe Arctic Security Cooperation Agreement
description: Negotiate and sign a new security cooperation agreement with European partners focused specifically on the shifting security landscape in the Arctic.
original_text: Forge a new Canada-Europe Arctic security cooperation agreement that deepens ties in the North in recognition of the rapidly shifting security landscape.
parent_title: Strengthen Arctic Sovereignty and Northern Infrastructure

title: Improve Support and Services for Canadian Veterans
description: A package of reforms aimed at modernizing benefits, improving health services, and enhancing civilian transition support for Canadian Veterans.
original_text: We owe the more than 460,000 Veterans living in Canada so much; the least we can do is make sure that they have the support they need and that their sacrifices are honoured. A Mark Carney-led government will:

title: Launch a Health Research Study on Women Veterans
description: Initiate a comprehensive health research study focused on the specific experiences and health outcomes of women Veterans transitioning to civilian life.
original_text: Improve health services and support for women Veterans’ health by launching a health research study on the experience of women Veterans to ensure proper data is collected so that its service delivery can capture the increase of women Veterans transitioning to civilian life and ultimately improve their health outcomes.
parent_title: Improve Support and Services for Canadian Veterans

title: Modernize the Veterans Benefits System to Reduce Wait Times
description: Overhaul the Veterans benefits processing system to reduce paperwork and accelerate decision-making for benefit claims.
original_text: Modernize and streamline the benefits system, to deliver faster decisions, shorter wait times, and less paperwork so that Veterans and their families get the support they need sooner and more easily.
parent_title: Improve Support and Services for Canadian Veterans

title: Streamline Civilian Recognition of Military Trade Credentials
description: Collaborate with provincial and territorial governments to ensure military trade credentials are automatically recognized in the civilian workforce, eliminating the need for retraining.
original_text: Make it easier for Veterans to get jobs by working with provinces and territories to streamline the recognition of military trade credentials in the civilian world, so that qualified Veterans do not needlessly retrain once they leave the military.
parent_title: Improve Support and Services for Canadian Veterans

title: Review Military Mission Designations for Enhanced Service Recognition
description: Conduct a formal review to properly designate past military missions (e.g., Persian Gulf) to ensure Veterans receive appropriate recognition for service in conflict zones.
original_text: Expand recognition of service by reviewing the designation of certain military missions, including in the Persian Gulf, so that Veterans’ sacrifices in conflict zones are properly recognized.
parent_title: Improve Support and Services for Canadian Veterans

title: Expand Culturally Relevant Services for Indigenous Veterans
description: Work with First Nation, Inuit, and Métis groups to develop and expand culturally appropriate support services for Indigenous Veterans.
original_text: Expand culturally relevant services for Indigenous Veterans in consultation with First Nation, Inuit, and Métis peoples.
parent_title: Improve Support and Services for Canadian Veterans

title: Assume Ownership of the National Field of Honour in Montreal
description: Partner with the Last Post Fund to assume ownership and ensure the long-term preservation of the National Field of Honour cemetery in Montreal.
original_text: Preserve the National Field of Honour through work with the Last Post Fund to assume ownership of the National Field of Honour in Montreal, so that it remains an appropriate and respectful final resting place for Veterans.
parent_title: Improve Support and Services for Canadian Veterans

title: Protect the Economy and Strategic Industries from Trade and Security Threats
description: A suite of economic defense measures designed to protect Canadian industries from U.S. tariffs, boost domestic manufacturing, and secure critical supply chains.
original_text: Our workers and businesses are under threat from America’s unjustified trade war... A Mark Carney-led government will:

title: Reinvest U.S. Tariff Revenues into Affected Canadian Industries
description: Direct all revenue generated from retaliatory tariffs against the U.S. to directly subsidize and support Canadian workers and businesses impacted by the trade dispute.
original_text: Respond to existing U.S. tariffs on our economy for as long as is necessary... Every dollar raised from these tariffs will support Canadian workers and businesses affected by the trade war.
parent_title: Protect the Economy and Strategic Industries from Trade and Security Threats

title: Create a $2 Billion Strategic Response Fund for the Auto Sector
description: Establish a new $2 billion fund to protect manufacturing jobs, upskill workers, and strengthen the domestic automotive supply chain.
original_text: Create a $2 billion Strategic Response Fund to boost our auto sector’s competitiveness, protect manufacturing jobs, support workers to upskill their expertise in the industry, and build a fortified Canadian supply chain – from raw materials to finished vehicles.
parent_title: Protect the Economy and Strategic Industries from Trade and Security Threats

title: Establish an All-in-Canada Network for Auto Component Manufacturing
description: Collaborate with the auto industry to localize component manufacturing, reducing cross-border supply chain reliance during vehicle production.
original_text: Build an “All-in-Canada” network for auto manufacturing component parts. With this network, we will work with industry to build more car parts in Canada, limit those parts crossing the border during production, and attract investment into our own industry.
parent_title: Protect the Economy and Strategic Industries from Trade and Security Threats

title: Strengthen the Investment Canada Act to Protect Core Sectors and Data Sovereignty
description: Amend the Investment Canada Act to expand transaction reviews, capture manipulative models, protect data sovereignty, and subject healthcare infrastructure acquisitions to strict oversight.
original_text: Safeguard our economy and our values and protect Canada from attempts to buy up our businesses... by strengthening the Investment Canada Act. We will make more transactions reviewable, as well as modernizing standards to capture manipulative transaction models – including by reviewing the potential impacts to Canada’s data sovereignty. We will also make clear that any transactions involving core infrastructure in our public health system... will be subject to stringent review and protection under the Act.
parent_title: Protect the Economy and Strategic Industries from Trade and Security Threats

title: Strengthen Food Security and Supply Chain Resilience
description: A collection of initiatives to lower food costs, increase domestic production capacity, and modify regulatory mandates to prioritize food security.
original_text: Food security is about more than just ensuring there is enough food on our plates, it’s also about protecting Canada’s economic sovereignty... A Mark Carney-led government will:

title: Invest in Controlled Environment Agriculture Facilities
description: Provide funding for controlled environment agriculture projects (like greenhouses and hydroponics) to increase domestic food production capabilities.
original_text: Strengthen Canada’s food security by investing in greenhouses, hydroponics, and other controlled environment agriculture that allow us to grow more types of food, here at home. We will work with the private sector to protect Canadian food security.
parent_title: Strengthen Food Security and Supply Chain Resilience

title: Amend Regulatory Mandates to Include Food Security and Cost Considerations
description: Change the statutory mandates of the CFIA and Pest Management Regulatory Agency to require them to factor food security and consumer costs into their regulatory decisions, alongside health and safety.
original_text: Amend the mandates of the Canadian Food Inspection Agency and the Pest Management Regulatory Agency to ensure they consider food security and the cost of food in all their regulatory decisions without compromising on health and safety.
parent_title: Strengthen Food Security and Supply Chain Resilience

title: Direct the Canada Infrastructure Bank to Prioritize Agri-Food Investments
description: Instruct the Canada Infrastructure Bank to focus its investments on projects related to agriculture, fisheries, and the broader food supply chain to reduce consumer costs.
original_text: Direct the Canada Infrastructure Bank to prioritize investments in agriculture, fisheries, agri-food, and the food supply chain, particularly those that improve food security and reduce the cost of food for Canadian families, unlocking potentially billions in investment for the sector.
parent_title: Strengthen Food Security and Supply Chain Resilience

title: Extend the Extended Railway Interswitching Pilot by Three Years
description: Prolong the Extended Railway Interswitching pilot program for three additional years to lower transport costs for farmers in Alberta, Manitoba, and Saskatchewan, while exploring the exclusion of non-Canadian railways.
original_text: Help Canadian farmers get their goods to market at low cost, particularly in Alberta, Manitoba, and Saskatchewan. We will do this by extending the Extended Railway Interswitching pilot by three years.
