title: Establish a 12% Target for Francophone Immigration Outside Quebec by 2029
description: Sets a measurable target to reach 12% Francophone immigration outside of Quebec by 2029, representing a 20% increase from current targets.
original_text: Establish a 12% target for Francophone immigration outside of Quebec by 2029, a 20% increase from current targets.
parent_title: Promote and Defend Canada's Official Languages

title: Support Entrepreneurs in Official Language Minority Communities
description: Commits to facilitating access to Francophone and bilingual workers to help entrepreneurs in Official Language Minority Communities scale their businesses.
original_text: Support entrepreneurs in Official Language Minority Communities looking to scale their businesses, notably by facilitating access to Francophone and bilingual workers.
parent_title: Promote and Defend Canada's Official Languages

title: Invest in Community Spaces for Official Language Minorities
description: Pledges ongoing financial investments into community spaces for linguistic minorities across Canada.
original_text: Continue to invest in community spaces for English-speaking minorities in Quebec and French speaking minorities across the country.
parent_title: Promote and Defend Canada's Official Languages

title: Create Scholarships and Invest in French-Language Education
description: Commits to creating a scholarship for Francophone students studying in minority contexts and collaborating with provinces to invest in French-language educational and community spaces.
original_text: Promote French-language educational pursuits across Canada by collaborating with provinces and territories to invest in educational and community spaces, such as early learning and child care that serve official language minority communities; creating a scholarship for Francophone students who continue their post-secondary studies in French in a minority context.
parent_title: Promote and Defend Canada's Official Languages

title: Protect and Modernize CBC/Radio-Canada
description: Promises to protect and modernize the public broadcaster to ensure continued bilingual programming.
original_text: Protect and modernize CBC/Radio-Canada to ensure that it continues to provide vital programming in both French and English.
parent_title: Promote and Defend Canada's Official Languages

title: Reduce the Minimum RRIF Withdrawal Amount by 25% for One Year
description: Provides a one-year, 25% reduction in the minimum withdrawal requirement for Registered Retirement Income Funds to protect seniors' savings during market volatility.
original_text: Protect Canadians' retirement savings from volatile market conditions by reducing the minimum amount that must be withdrawn from a Registered Retirement Income Fund (RRIF) by 25% for one year. This gives seniors much-needed flexibility to avoid liquidating their retirement savings in a down market to meet current RRIF rules.

title: Increase the Guaranteed Income Supplement by 5%
description: Increases the Guaranteed Income Supplement by 5%, providing a direct tax-free income boost of up to $652 annually for low-income seniors.
original_text: Give a temporary income boost to low-income seniors by increasing the Guaranteed Income Supplement (GIS) by 5%, providing up to an additional $652 in income per year, tax-free.

title: Support Young Canadians Through Employment, Housing, and Mental Health Initiatives
description: Groups commitments aimed at supporting young Canadians through employment programs, climate action initiatives, mental health funding, and housing investments.
original_text: A Mark Carney-led government will: Get young Canadians started with their first job... Establish a Youth Climate Corps pilot... Continue to uphold the removal of interest on Canada Student Loans... Make generational housing investments... Offer the Canada Strong Pass... Provide 100,000 young people a year with mental health care... Provide a new Apprenticeship Grant

title: Expand Funding and Mandates for Youth Employment Programs
description: Commits to providing funding and expanding mandates for key youth employment programs to align with critical sectors like green technology, AI, and advanced manufacturing.
original_text: Provide funding and expand the mandates, where appropriate, for the Student Work Placement Program, the Youth Employment and Skills Strategy (YESS), Canada Summer Jobs, and the Canadian Service Corps. We will work to better align the mandates with critical and emerging sectors such as green technology, artificial intelligence, and advanced manufacturing.
parent_title: Support Young Canadians Through Employment, Housing, and Mental Health Initiatives

title: Establish a Youth Climate Corps Pilot
description: Creates a new pilot program to provide paid skills training for youth specifically focused on climate emergency response and community resilience.
original_text: Establish a Youth Climate Corps pilot which will provide paid skills training for young Canadians to quickly respond to climate emergencies, support recovery, and strengthen resilience in communities across the country.
parent_title: Support Young Canadians Through Employment, Housing, and Mental Health Initiatives

title: Maintain Zero Interest on Canada Student and Apprentice Loans
description: Maintains the elimination of interest charges on federal student and apprentice loans.
original_text: Continue to uphold the removal of interest on Canada Student Loans and Canada Apprentice Loans.
parent_title: Support Young Canadians Through Employment, Housing, and Mental Health Initiatives

title: Double the Pace of Housing Construction
description: Sets an outcome-oriented goal to double the pace of housing construction through generational investments to lower costs for young Canadians.
original_text: Make generational housing investments to double the pace of construction and drive down the cost of homes, rent, and mortgage payments.
parent_title: Support Young Canadians Through Employment, Housing, and Mental Health Initiatives

title: Introduce the Canada Strong Pass for Young Canadians
description: Introduces a new pass to encourage youth travel and discovery within Canada over the summer.
original_text: Offer the Canada Strong Pass so young Canadians can discover Canada this summer [more].
parent_title: Support Young Canadians Through Employment, Housing, and Mental Health Initiatives

title: Establish a Permanent Youth Mental Health Fund to Support 100,000 Youth Annually
description: Establishes a measurable target to provide mental health care to 100,000 young people annually through a new permanent Youth Mental Health Fund.
original_text: Provide 100,000 young people a year with mental health care by establishing a permanent Youth Mental Health Fund [more].
parent_title: Support Young Canadians Through Employment, Housing, and Mental Health Initiatives

title: Provide a New Apprenticeship Grant of up to $8,000
description: Creates a new direct financial grant of up to $8,000 specifically for registered apprentices.
original_text: Provide a new Apprenticeship Grant of up to $8,000 for registered apprentices [more].
parent_title: Support Young Canadians Through Employment, Housing, and Mental Health Initiatives

title: Uphold the Charter and Advance Equal Rights
description: Groups commitments focused on expanding human rights, combating discrimination, and reforming the justice system for persons with disabilities.
original_text: A Mark Carney-led government will: Stand up for the Canadian Charter of Rights and Freedoms... Enable Canadians to take more action to affirm their rights by expanding the Court Challenges Program... Build a Canada where everyone has a fair shot... Confront systemic barriers... Make funding permanent for the 2SLGBTQI+ Community Capacity Fund... Review and reform the process to apply for the Disability Tax Credit... Guarantee equal access to justice for people with disabilities by developing a Disability Justice Framework
source_section: Party of the Charter, Party of Equal Rights

title: Expand the Court Challenges Program
description: Commits to expanding the Court Challenges Program, which provides financial support for Canadians taking legal action to affirm their Charter rights.
original_text: Enable Canadians to take more action to affirm their rights by expanding the Court Challenges Program.
source_section: Party of the Charter, Party of Equal Rights

title: Make the 2SLGBTQI+ Community Capacity Fund Permanent
description: Converts the existing 2SLGBTQI+ Community Capacity Fund into a permanently funded program to support rights organizations.
original_text: Make funding permanent for the 2SLGBTQI+ Community Capacity Fund to support community organizations and networks that protect rights and advance equality across Canada.
source_section: Party of the Charter, Party of Equal Rights
parent_title: Uphold the Charter and Advance Equal Rights

title: Reform the Disability Tax Credit Application Process
description: Initiates a review and reform of the application process for the Disability Tax Credit, including consideration to expand eligibility criteria for additional impairments.
original_text: Review and reform the process to apply for the Disability Tax Credit (DTC) now that the DTC has become the gateway to key federal programs like the Canada Disability Benefit, the CCB for children with disabilities, the dental benefit, and various other programs. We will also consider expanding the eligibility criteria to include additional impairments.
source_section: Party of the Charter, Party of Equal Rights
parent_title: Uphold the Charter and Advance Equal Rights

title: Develop a Disability Justice Framework
description: Commits to developing a new framework to address systemic discrimination against persons with disabilities within the justice system.
original_text: Guarantee equal access to justice for people with disabilities by developing a Disability Justice Framework to address long-standing inequities, biases, and systemic discrimination against persons with disabilities within the justice system and before the law.
source_section: Party of the Charter, Party of Equal Rights

title: Protect Women's Health, Rights, and Economic Prosperity
description: Groups commitments aiming to secure women's reproductive health care, research, and economic opportunities.
original_text: A Mark Carney-led government will: Make the Sexual and Reproductive Health Fund Program permanent... Establish a new in vitro fertilization (IVF) program... Address the under-researched area of postpartum maternal health... Invest in increased collection of data to help address women's health gaps... Work to end gender-based violence... Unlock new economic opportunities for women... Protect and strengthen child care in Canada.

title: Make the Sexual and Reproductive Health Fund Program Permanent
description: Secures permanent ongoing funding for the Sexual and Reproductive Health Fund to protect access to abortion care.
original_text: Make the Sexual and Reproductive Health Fund Program permanent, so that there will always be funding for abortion care in Canada, with access to reproductive care protected for underserved communities [more].
parent_title: Protect Women's Health, Rights, and Economic Prosperity

title: Establish a New $20,000 In Vitro Fertilization (IVF) Program
description: Establishes a new funding program providing up to $20,000 for a single standard cycle of IVF treatment, working in coordination with provinces and territories.
original_text: Establish a new in vitro fertilization (IVF) program that provides up to $20,000 for a single standard cycle of IVF treatment, making it more affordable for Canadians who want to become parents, especially 2SLGBTQI+ Canadians who face a costly journey to parenthood. We will work with the provinces and territories to make sure more Canadians can access this support.
parent_title: Protect Women's Health, Rights, and Economic Prosperity

title: Invest in Postpartum Maternal Health Research
description: Commits research funding specifically to study outcomes, disparities, and service gaps in postpartum maternal health.
original_text: Address the under-researched area of postpartum maternal health by investing in research into postpartum maternal health to fund studies and track outcomes, disparities, and service gaps, and inform future investments so that women get better care after giving birth.
parent_title: Protect Women's Health, Rights, and Economic Prosperity

title: Increase Data Collection on Women's Health Gaps
description: Promises investments to increase data collection on understudied areas of women's health such as menopause, endometriosis, and maternal mortality.
original_text: Invest in increased collection of data to help address women's health gaps in Canada, particularly in understudied areas of menopause, endometriosis, maternal mortality and morbidity, stillbirth, and perinatal health.
parent_title: Protect Women's Health, Rights, and Economic Prosperity

title: Continue Investments in the National Action Plan to End Gender-Based Violence
description: Commits to continued funding for the National Action Plan to End Gender-Based Violence, prioritizing Indigenous-led approaches and support for victims.
original_text: Work to end gender-based violence by continuing investments in the National Action Plan to End Gender-Based Violence so that we can support victims and survivors, prevent violence from happening in the first place, and support Indigenous-led approaches that will be prioritized in partnership.
parent_title: Protect Women's Health, Rights, and Economic Prosperity

title: Increase Support for the Women's Economic and Leadership Opportunities Fund
description: Increases financial support for a specific fund designed to promote women's economic security and leadership.
original_text: Unlock new economic opportunities for women by increasing support for the Women's Economic and Leadership Opportunities Fund. This fund invests in projects that promote women's economic security and prosperity.
parent_title: Protect Women's Health, Rights, and Economic Prosperity

title: Advance Reconciliation and Indigenous Self-Determination
description: Groups commitments to advance self-determination, implement treaties, and respond to key truth and reconciliation calls to action.
original_text: A Mark Carney-led government will: Work in full partnership... Support Indigenous-led processes for advancing self-determination... Work in partnership on the implementation of treaties... Commit to developing and supporting... structures that support a nation-to-nation... relationship... Move forward on the important work of the Truth and Reconciliation Commission's Calls to Action... Move forward on the implementation of the National Inquiry into Missing and Murdered Indigenous Women and Girls Calls for Justice... Implement the United Nations Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples Act

title: Support Indigenous-Led Processes for Self-Determination and Jurisdiction
description: Supports Indigenous-led processes to transfer jurisdiction and self-determination in areas like education, health, child/family services, and policing.
original_text: Support Indigenous-led processes for advancing self-determination and the exercise of jurisdiction in priority areas such as education, health, child and family services as well as community safety and policing.
parent_title: Advance Reconciliation and Indigenous Self-Determination

title: Implement Treaties, Land Claims, and Self-Government Agreements
description: Commits to advancing the implementation of treaties, land claims, and self-government agreements in partnership with Indigenous peoples.
original_text: Work in partnership on the implementation of treaties, land claim, and self-government agreements.
parent_title: Advance Reconciliation and Indigenous Self-Determination

title: Develop Structures for Nation-to-Nation Relationships
description: Commits to developing new institutional structures to facilitate nation-to-nation, Inuit-Crown, and government-to-government relationships.
original_text: Commit to developing and supporting, in partnership, the structures that support a nation-to-nation, Inuit-Crown, government-to-government relationship to progress on shared priorities.
parent_title: Advance Reconciliation and Indigenous Self-Determination

title: Support TRC Calls to Action and Uncovering of Unmarked Graves
description: Pledges continued progress on TRC Calls to Action and explicitly supports efforts to uncover undocumented graves at residential schools.
original_text: Move forward on the important work of the Truth and Reconciliation Commission's Calls to Action and support Indigenous communities to uncover unmarked and undocumented graves and burial sites at residential schools.
parent_title: Advance Reconciliation and Indigenous Self-Determination

title: Implement MMIWG Calls for Justice and National Action Plan
description: Commits to implementing the Calls for Justice from the National Inquiry into MMIWG and the associated National Action Plan.
original_text: Move forward on the implementation of the National Inquiry into Missing and Murdered Indigenous Women and Girls Calls for Justice and the National Action Plan.
parent_title: Advance Reconciliation and Indigenous Self-Determination

title: Implement the UNDRIP Act and Action Plan
description: Reaffirms the commitment to implement the UNDRIP Act and its corresponding Action Plan.
original_text: Implement the United Nations Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples Act and the Action Plan.
parent_title: Advance Reconciliation and Indigenous Self-Determination

title: Expand Indigenous Lifelong Education and Training Opportunities
description: Groups commitments to expand educational and skills training programs for Indigenous peoples, including a new pathways fund and accelerated post-secondary funding.
original_text: A Mark Carney-led government will: Launch a new Indigenous Pathways to Prosperity Skills and Training Fund... Accelerate funding for access to post-secondary education... Protect K-12 education on-reserve

title: Launch the Indigenous Pathways to Prosperity Skills and Training Fund
description: Creates a new fund to support partnerships between Indigenous communities and educational institutions for skills training in priority areas like healthcare and teaching.
original_text: Launch a new Indigenous Pathways to Prosperity Skills and Training Fund which will support partnerships between First Nations, Inuit, and Métis communities and organizations, schools, training centres, and colleges to advance access to skills training and education opportunities. This will support the training of more First Nations, Inuit, and Métis doctors, teachers, and builders, amongst other priority areas identified by partners.
parent_title: Expand Indigenous Lifelong Education and Training Opportunities

title: Accelerate Funding for Indigenous Post-Secondary Education and Infrastructure
description: Increases funding to remove cost barriers for Indigenous students accessing post-secondary education and invests in Indigenous-led institutions like Inuit Nunangat University and First Nations University.
original_text: Accelerate funding for access to post-secondary education so more First Nations, Inuit, and Métis students can go to school and invest in associated infrastructure. Cost should not the barrier. We will also invest in Indigenous-led post-secondary education institutions such as the Inuit Nunangat University and the First Nations University.
parent_title: Expand Indigenous Lifelong Education and Training Opportunities

title: Protect K-12 Education On-Reserve
description: Commits to protecting K-12 education systems on-reserve to ensure cultural and linguistic continuity for First Nations children.
original_text: Protect K-12 education on-reserve so that more First Nations kids can go to school surrounded by their language, culture, and communities.
parent_title: Expand Indigenous Lifelong Education and Training Opportunities

title: Build Stronger and Healthier Indigenous Communities
description: Groups commitments focused on Indigenous economic reconciliation, infrastructure, housing, clean water, language revitalization, health, and climate adaptation.
original_text: A Mark Carney-led government will: Expand the Indigenous Loan Guarantee Program... Build together with First Nations, Inuit, and Métis communities the critical infrastructure needed... Increase lands added to reserves... Immediately introduce and pass legislation affirming that First Nations have a human right to clean drinking water... Revitalize Indigenous languages... Increase investments for Indigenous mental health... Co-develop evidence-based food security approaches... Double First Nation, Inuit, and Métis capacity funding... Establish a new Indigenous Climate Readiness and Adaptation Fund... Work with First Nation, Inuit, and Métis peoples to advance economic reconciliation

title: Expand the Indigenous Loan Guarantee Program to $10 Billion
description: Doubles the financial capacity of the Indigenous Loan Guarantee Program to $10 billion and expands its sectoral scope to increase Indigenous participation in resource projects.
original_text: Expand the Indigenous Loan Guarantee Program from $5 billion to $10 billion and increase the sectoral scope. This will enable a greater share in the benefits of natural resources and energy projects in Indigenous Peoples' territories, on their own terms.
parent_title: Build Stronger and Healthier Indigenous Communities

title: Expand Canada Infrastructure Bank Support for Indigenous Projects
description: Broadens the types of projects the Canada Infrastructure Bank is authorized to support to better align with Indigenous priorities.
original_text: Expand the kinds of projects the Canada Infrastructure Bank can support to be more in line with First Nation, Inuit, and Métis priorities.
parent_title: Build Stronger and Healthier Indigenous Communities

title: Explore Options for an Indigenous Infrastructure Bank
description: Commits to exploring the creation of a dedicated Indigenous Infrastructure Bank to improve access to capital for Indigenous communities.
original_text: Explore options for an Indigenous Infrastructure Bank to address access to capital.

title: Determine Path for First Nations 10-Year Infrastructure Plans
description: Commits to working out a framework for long-term (10-year) infrastructure planning for First Nations to provide predictable funding.
original_text: Determine the best path forward towards First Nations 10-year infrastructure plans.
parent_title: Build Stronger and Healthier Indigenous Communities

title: Realize First Nations Control Over Infrastructure
description: Transfers control of infrastructure directly to First Nations using models like the First Nations Atlantic Water Authority.
original_text: Realize First Nations control over First Nations infrastructure wherever possible through models like the First Nations Atlantic Water Authority.
parent_title: Build Stronger and Healthier Indigenous Communities

title: Support Indigenous Housing Construction
description: Directly funds and supports the construction of housing in urban, rural, and northern Indigenous communities in partnership with Indigenous housing providers.
original_text: Work in partnership with Indigenous communities and Indigenous housing providers to directly support building housing including in urban, rural, and northern Indigenous communities.
parent_title: Build Stronger and Healthier Indigenous Communities

title: Increase Lands Added to Reserves Within Four Years
description: Sets a timeline to accelerate and increase the amount of land added to First Nations reserves over the next four years.
original_text: Increase lands added to reserves within the next four years and work with First Nations to accelerate additions to reserve.
parent_title: Build Stronger and Healthier Indigenous Communities

title: Introduce Legislation Affirming First Nations' Right to Clean Drinking Water
description: Promises to pass immediate legislation legally recognizing that First Nations have a human right to clean drinking water.
original_text: Immediately introduce and pass legislation affirming that First Nations have a human right to clean drinking water.
parent_title: Build Stronger and Healthier Indigenous Communities

title: Support Projects to Revitalize Indigenous Languages
description: Funds community-driven projects aimed at restoring, protecting, and promoting Indigenous languages.
original_text: Revitalize Indigenous languages by supporting community-driven projects that restore, protect, and promote this important part of First Nations, Inuit, and Métis cultures and histories.
parent_title: Build Stronger and Healthier Indigenous Communities

title: Increase Investments for Indigenous Mental Health and Wellness Centres
description: Increases financial investments directed toward Indigenous mental health programs, healing and wellness centres, and friendship centres.
original_text: Increase investments for Indigenous mental health, healing and wellness centres, and friendship centres.
parent_title: Build Stronger and Healthier Indigenous Communities

title: Co-develop Food Security Approaches to Replace Nutrition North Canada
description: Commits to replacing the Nutrition North Canada program by co-developing new evidence-based food security approaches with Inuit and Northern Indigenous leaders to lower prices.
original_text: Co-develop evidence-based food security approaches to replace Nutrition North Canada with Inuit and Northern Indigenous leaders. The affordability crisis that all Canadians are feeling is even more extreme in Northern communities. Concrete action that brings real results must be taken to bring down prices and increase access.
parent_title: Build Stronger and Healthier Indigenous Communities

title: Double Capacity Funding for Indigenous Major Project Assessments
description: Doubles the funding provided to Indigenous groups to build capacity for participating in major project assessments and consultations.
original_text: Double First Nation, Inuit, and Métis capacity funding to contribute to major project assessments for more consistent, fair, and meaningful consultations [more].
parent_title: Build Stronger and Healthier Indigenous Communities

title: Advance Economic Reconciliation and Enable Indigenous-Led Initiatives
description: Commits to advancing economic reconciliation through systemic reforms that enable and support Indigenous-led economic initiatives.
original_text: Work with First Nation, Inuit, and Métis peoples to advance economic reconciliation, including reforms that enable Indigenous-led initiatives.
parent_title: Build Stronger and Healthier Indigenous Communities

title: Partner with Indigenous Communities to Build Critical Infrastructure
description: A broad commitment to collaborate with Indigenous communities on critical infrastructure, focusing on creating predictable funding cycles and aligning priority setting with community needs. This serves as an umbrella for specific infrastructure mechanisms.
original_text: Build together with First Nations, Inuit, and Métis communities the critical infrastructure needed all across the country. Predictable funding cycles and getting priority setting closer to community needs is critical to increasing the pace of work and the effectiveness of infrastructure funding. To accelerate this work, we will:

title: Expand Canada Infrastructure Bank Project Scope for Indigenous Priorities
description: Modifies the mandate of the Canada Infrastructure Bank to ensure supported projects align with the priorities of First Nation, Inuit, and Métis communities.
original_text: Expand the kinds of projects the Canada Infrastructure Bank can support to be more in line with First Nation, Inuit, and Métis priorities.
parent_title: Partner with Indigenous Communities to Build Critical Infrastructure

title: Explore Options for an Indigenous Infrastructure Bank
description: Commits to reviewing and exploring the feasibility of creating a dedicated Indigenous Infrastructure Bank to improve access to capital for Indigenous communities.
original_text: Explore options for an Indigenous Infrastructure Bank to address access to capital.
parent_title: Partner with Indigenous Communities to Build Critical Infrastructure

title: Continue Support for Indigenous Early Learning and Child Care
description: Maintains ongoing financial and structural support for Indigenous Early Learning and Child Care programs.
original_text: Continue to support Indigenous Early Learning and Child Care.

title: Eliminate Tuberculosis in Inuit Nunangat by 2030
description: Sets a specific, measurable target to completely eliminate tuberculosis in the Inuit Nunangat region by the year 2030.
original_text: Continue to partner with Inuit to eliminate tuberculosis in Inuit Nunangat by 2030.

title: Continue Funding for Jordan’s Principle
description: Maintains ongoing federal funding for Jordan's Principle to ensure First Nations children receive necessary health, social, and educational products and services.
original_text: Continue to fund Jordan’s Principle.

title: Continue Funding for the Inuit Child First Initiative
description: Maintains ongoing federal funding for the Inuit Child First Initiative to ensure Inuit children have access to essential government-funded health, social, and educational products.
original_text: Continue to fund Inuit Child First Initiative.

title: Continue Indigenous Child Welfare Reform
description: Commits to continuing ongoing systemic reforms to the Indigenous child welfare system.
original_text: Continue the work on child welfare reform.

title: Reduce the Lowest Marginal Income Tax Rate by 1 Percentage Point by July 2025
description: Reduces the marginal tax rate on the lowest income bracket by 1 percentage point, effective July 1, 2025, aimed at saving middle-class and dual-income families up to $825 annually.
original_text: Cut income taxes for the middle class and save dual-income families up to $825 a year. We will reduce the marginal tax rate on the lowest tax bracket by 1 percentage point. A tax cut for the middle class means millions of hard-working Canadians will be able to keep more of what they earn and build a stronger future. This will come into effect on Canada Day 2025.

title: Eliminate GST for First-Time Homebuyers on Homes Up to $1 Million
description: Eliminates the Goods and Services Tax (GST) for first-time homebuyers purchasing homes valued up to $1 million, saving buyers up to $50,000.
original_text: Cut the GST for first-time homebuyers on homes up to $1 million. Canadians will save up to $50,000, allowing more young people and families to achieve their dream of homeownership. This will lower upfront costs so Canadians can keep more of their hard-earned money.

title: Lower GST for First-Time Homebuyers on Homes Between $1 Million and $1.5 Million
description: Provides a partial reduction in the Goods and Services Tax (GST) for first-time homebuyers purchasing properties priced between $1 million and $1.5 million in expensive housing markets.
original_text: In recognition of the high costs of homes in more expensive housing markets, we will also lower the GST on homes between $1 million and $1.5 million for first-time buyers.
parent_title: Eliminate GST for First-Time Homebuyers on Homes Up to $1 Million

title: Deliver Automatic Tax Filing for Low-Income Households and Seniors
description: Implements an automatic tax filing system specifically targeting low-income households and seniors to ensure they receive eligible benefits like the Canada Child Benefit and GST/HST credit.
original_text: Get Canadians the benefits they deserve by delivering automatic tax filing starting with low-income households and seniors. This will ensure more Canadians can access the benefits they are entitled to such as the Canada Child Benefit and GST/HST credit, both benefits which help lift Canadians out of poverty.

title: Review the Mortgage Market to Support Longer Interest Rate Terms
description: Initiates a review of the Canadian mortgage market to explore removing barriers to longer interest rate terms, aiming to provide financial stability for homeowners.
original_text: Make mortgage payments more affordable by reviewing Canada’s mortgage market with the objective to offer Canadians more options while retaining stability in the market. This work will look at barriers to longer interest rate terms on mortgages, which would give Canadians more financial stability.

title: Fund Home Retrofits and Heat Pumps for Low- and Middle-Income Households
description: Provides financial support for home retrofits, specifically targeting low- and middle-income households and renters to adopt heat pumps, insulation, and window replacements.
original_text: Fund home retrofits and lower utility bills while making it easier for low- and middle-income households, including renters, to adopt heat pumps and energy efficiency upgrades. Eligible costs could include insulation upgrades, heat pump installation, and window replacements.

title: Fund Household Flood and Wildfire Adaptation Measures via Carbon Credit Market
description: Provides funding to families for climate adaptation measures (e.g., roof repair, sump pumps, foundation sealing) to mitigate flood and wildfire risks, financed through a reformed carbon credit market.
original_text: Lower household risks to floods and wildfires, directly helping Canadian families to protect against floods, wildfires, and other extreme weather events. This will help with funding towards costly adaptation measures such as repairing or replacing your roof, installing a sump pump, or sealing cracks in your foundations. We will use a reformed carbon credit market to finance these climate conscious choices, meaning that big polluters will pay.

title: Amend the Greenhouse Gas Pollution Pricing Act to Repeal the Consumer Carbon Tax by March 2025
description: Formally amends the Greenhouse Gas Pollution Pricing Act in March 2025 to permanently repeal the consumer carbon tax, shifting the burden to large industrial polluters.
original_text: Amend the Greenhouse Gas Pollution Pricing Act to legislate the repeal of the consumer “carbon tax” after Mark Carney's government cancelled it on Day One, in March 2025. We will do this while making sure big polluters pay and delivering a bold plan to reduce emissions and drive investments in clean technologies.

title: Create Build Canada Homes (BCH) to Develop Federal Housing
description: Establishes a new federal entity, Build Canada Homes (BCH), to re-engage the federal government directly in housing development and construction.
original_text: Create Build Canada Homes (BCH) to get the federal government back into the business of building homes.

title: Act as a Federal Developer for Affordable Housing on Public Lands
description: Through Build Canada Homes, the federal government will act as a direct developer to build housing at scale, utilizing public lands and partnering with private builders for construction.
original_text: Act as a developer to build affordable housing at scale, including on public lands. BCH will develop and manage projects and partner with builders for the construction phase of projects.
parent_title: Create Build Canada Homes (BCH) to Develop Federal Housing

title: Provide $25 Billion in Financing to Prefabricated Home Builders
description: Commits over $25 billion in financing through Build Canada Homes to support prefabricated home builders using Canadian mass timber and modular technologies, including issuing bulk orders.
original_text: Build faster, smarter, sustainable, more affordable homes by providing over $25 billion in financing to innovative prefabricated home builders in Canada, including those using Canadian technologies and resources like mass timber and softwood lumber. BCH will also issue bulk orders of units from manufacturers to create sustained demand. This will revitalize how we build homes in Canada, bringing forestry, innovation, engineering, manufacturing, and construction together.
parent_title: Create Build Canada Homes (BCH) to Develop Federal Housing

title: Inject $10 Billion in Low-Cost Financing for Middle- and Low-Income Housing
description: Injects $10 billion in low-cost financing and capital through Build Canada Homes specifically targeted at building affordable housing for middle- and low-income Canadians, students, seniors, veterans, and Indigenous peoples.
original_text: Support affordable homebuilders by injecting $10 billion in low-cost financing and capital for homes that support middle- and low-income Canadians. This will include housing for students, seniors, Veterans, people with disabilities, and Indigenous housing, shelters, and more.
parent_title: Create Build Canada Homes (BCH) to Develop Federal Housing

title: Prioritize Sustainable Building Materials in Federal Housing Projects
description: Mandates that Build Canada Homes prioritize sustainable building practices, including using sustainably sourced wood and low-emissions materials.
original_text: Prioritize -sustainable building including sustainably sourced wood, recycled content, and low-emissions materials.
parent_title: Create Build Canada Homes (BCH) to Develop Federal Housing

title: Mandate Apprentice Hiring in Major Project Federal Contribution Agreements
description: Introduces new requirements on federal contribution agreements for major projects to mandate that industry partners hire apprentices and recent graduates.
original_text: Incentivize companies to hire apprentices and recent graduates by establishing new requirements on federal contribution agreements to major projects that commit industry partners to include significant opportunities for young Canadians.
parent_title: Create Build Canada Homes (BCH) to Develop Federal Housing

title: Halve Municipal Development Charges for Multi-Unit Residential Housing for Five Years
description: Reduces municipal development charges by 50% for multi-unit residential housing over five years, offsetting the lost municipal revenue with federal investments in local infrastructure (water, power, wastewater).
original_text: Cut municipal development charges in half for multi-unit residential housing for five years by working with provinces and territories to keep municipalities whole. These revenues will be offset by federal investment in housing infrastructure like water, power lines, and wastewater systems.

title: Reintroduce the Multi-Unit Rental Building (MURB) Tax Incentive
description: Reinstates the Multi-Unit Rental Building (MURB) tax incentive to spur the construction of new rental housing units.
original_text: Reintroduce a tax incentive for home builders known as the Multi-Unit Rental Building (MURB), which, in the 1970s, spurred tens of thousands of rental housing units across the country.

title: Facilitate Conversion of Existing Structures into Affordable Housing
description: Commits to easing regulations or providing support to convert existing non-residential structures into affordable housing units.
original_text: Facilitate the conversion of existing structures into affordable housing units.

title: Expand the Housing Accelerator Fund and Publicly Report Municipal Permitting Timelines
description: Expands the Housing Accelerator Fund to further cut zoning restrictions and bureaucracy, and implements public reporting on municipal permitting and approval timelines.
original_text: Build on the success of the Housing Accelerator Fund, further reducing housing bureaucracy, zoning restrictions, and other red tape so that builders only need to navigate one housing market, instead of thirteen. And we will publicly report on municipalities’ progress to speed up permitting and approval timelines.

title: Streamline Housing Approvals and Simplify National Building Codes
description: Implements a suite of regulatory reforms to speed up housing approvals, including simplifying national building codes, eliminating duplicative inspections, standardizing designs on public lands, and fast-tracking proven builders.
original_text: Speed up approvals by reforming and simplifying national building codes; eliminating duplicative inspections and streamlining regulations for prefabricated and modular housing; leveraging pre-approved, standardized housing designs across all public lands and encouraging the adoption of the designs as-of-right across the country; allowing builders and other orders of government to apply for multiple projects at once; and fast tracking builders who have a proven record with government.

title: Prohibit Federally Supported Housing in High-Risk Flood and Wildfire Zones
description: Prohibits the construction of federally supported housing in areas identified as high risk for floods and wildfires to reduce exposure to climate-related damages.
original_text: Build federal homes in ways that reduce the risk of costly damages. To protect homeowners and renters from costly flood and wildfire risks, federally supported housing will not be built in areas that are at high risk for floods and wildfires. Housing will be built in safer locations, reducing homeowners’ exposure to costly risks and protecting their health and wellbeing.

title: Establish a National High-Risk Flood Insurance Program by April 2026
description: Creates a national, low-cost flood insurance program by April 2026 to help homeowners mitigate climate-related financial risks.
original_text: Stand up Canada’s high-risk flood insurance program by April 2026 to support homeowners to reduce their exposure to future climate risk. We will expedite work with provinces, territories, and industry to finalize this low-cost program.

title: Cap Temporary Residents and International Students at Less Than 5% of the Population by 2027
description: Sets a hard cap on the combined number of temporary workers and international students at less than 5% of Canada's total population by the end of 2027.
original_text: Return immigration to sustainable levels by capping the total number of temporary workers and international students to less than 5% of Canada’s population by the end of 2027, from a past high of 7.3%, to ease the strain on housing, public infrastructure, and our social services.

title: Stabilize Permanent Resident Admissions at Less Than 1% of the Population Annually Beyond 2027
description: Caps permanent resident admissions at a maximum of 1% of Canada's total population annually starting beyond 2027, prioritizing economic growth and family reunification.
original_text: Stabilize permanent resident admissions at less than 1% of Canada’s population annually beyond 2027 with a focus on attracting top global talent that will drive economic growth and prosperity. Our immigration plan will also allow for the reunification of families, while maintaining Canada’s global leadership in prioritizing the world’s most vulnerable, including human rights defenders and refugees.

title: Revitalize the Global Skills Strategy to Attract Top Global Talent
description: Revamps the Global Skills Strategy to attract highly skilled global talent, specifically targeting professionals from the United States to support high-growth Canadian businesses.
original_text: Attract the best talent in the world to build our economy by revitalizing the Global Skills Strategy to help high-growth Canadian businesses and entrepreneurs with new projects that will support Canada’s strategic economic growth. This includes attracting highly skilled talent from the United States who can help grow the economy here in Canada.

title: Streamline Recognition of Foreign Credentials and Professional Experience
description: Collaborates with provinces and territories to accelerate the recognition of foreign credentials and professional experience for newcomers.
original_text: Work with provinces and territories to streamline and speed up recognition of foreign credentials and international professional experience, so that those who come to Canada can contribute their skills to the economy more quickly upon arrival.

title: Leverage Digital Tools to Eliminate Immigration Backlogs
description: Invests in digital service tools to eliminate immigration backlogs and reduce application processing times.
original_text: Work towards a fairer and faster process and enhance service delivery for applicants by leveraging digital service tools to reduce processing times and eliminate backlogs to ensure that our immigration system is more reliable for families, businesses, and applicants.

title: Support Legal Aid for Asylum Seekers and Refugees
description: Provides dedicated funding for legal aid services for asylum seekers and refugees to expedite case resolutions and reduce appeals.
original_text: Support legal aid for asylum seekers and refugees so that claimants receive timely advice and representation, have their cases resolved as quickly as possible, and reduce unnecessary appeals.

title: Enforce the Removal of Failed Asylum Claimants Following Due Process
description: Enforces the timely removal of failed asylum claimants after due process is completed to maintain the integrity of the immigration system.
original_text: Move forward with a credible and fair immigration system that removes failed claimants once due process has been accorded, reinforcing the system's integrity.

title: Increase Resources for Border Security Screenings and Immigration Enforcement
description: Increases funding and resources for border security screenings, enforcement against immigration fraud, and tightening visa requirements.
original_text: Strengthen the integrity of the border by increasing resources for security screenings, quickly identifying and removing inadmissible individuals, tightening visa requirements, and enhancing enforcement against immigration fraud.

title: Uphold the Canada-Québec Accord on Immigration Responsibilities
description: Affirms the commitment to the Canada-Québec Accord, respecting shared jurisdiction over immigration to protect the French language and Quebec's culture.
original_text: Continue to respect that immigration, both permanent and temporary, is a shared responsibility with the Government of Quebec. We share their commitment, within the Canada-Québec Accord, to responsible immigration levels that protect the French language and Quebec’s unique culture.

title: Enable Real-Time Information Sharing Between Governments on Immigration Status
description: Implements real-time information sharing systems between federal, provincial, and territorial governments regarding claimant status, removals, and settlement supports.
original_text: Strengthen federal, provincial, and territorial collaboration by enhancing cooperation between governments, agencies, and organizations to enable real-time information sharing on claimant status, removals, and settlement supports to improve efficiency and service delivery.

title: Create the First and Last Mile Fund (FLMF) for Critical Mineral Supply Chains
description: Creates the First and Last Mile Fund (FLMF) to connect critical mineral projects to broader supply chains and improve economic integration.
original_text: Connect critical mineral projects to supply chains through a new First and Last Mile Fund (FLMF). This will make more projects viable and create a more integrated and accessible Canadian economy.

title: Finance Clean Energy and Mineral Processing via the First and Last Mile Fund
description: Uses the newly created First and Last Mile Fund to directly finance clean energy and critical mineral projects, including on-site development, processing, and refining.
original_text: Directly support clean energy and critical mineral projects through the FLMF, reducing our reliance on other countries and protecting Canadian jobs. This will include supporting on-site development, processing, and refining capacity.

title: Invest in Critical Mineral Prospecting and End-of-Life Recovery
description: Invests in accelerating mineral exploration, prospecting, and the recovery of minerals from end-of-life products like batteries and mining waste to build a circular economy.
original_text: Accelerate exploration and extraction, including investing in prospecting activities and recovering valuable minerals from end-of-life products like mining waste and batteries, contributing to a circular economy.

title: Expand Existing Tax Credits for Critical Minerals Exploration and Extraction
description: A parent commitment to broaden and modify existing tax credits to attract and derisk investments in the exploration and extraction of critical minerals.
original_text: Attract, expand, and derisk investments in critical minerals exploration and extraction through the expansion of existing tax credits. We will do this by:

title: Broaden the Critical Mineral Exploration Tax Credit
description: Expands the Critical Mineral Exploration Tax Credit to cover a broader list of minerals essential for defence, semiconductors, and clean energy technologies.
original_text: Broadening the Critical Mineral Exploration Tax Credit by expanding qualifying minerals to include critical minerals necessary for defence, semiconductors, energy, and other clean technologies.
parent_title: Expand Existing Tax Credits for Critical Minerals Exploration and Extraction

title: Include Technical Study Costs in Canadian Exploration Expenses
description: Amends the definition of Canadian exploration expenses to allow the inclusion of costs related to technical, engineering, and economic feasibility studies for critical mineral projects.
original_text: Expanding eligible activities under Canadian exploration expenses to include the costs of technical studies, such as engineering, economic and feasibility studies for critical minerals projects.
parent_title: Expand Existing Tax Credits for Critical Minerals Exploration and Extraction

title: Modify the Clean Technology Manufacturing ITC for Brownfield Mine Development
description: Modifies the Clean Technology Manufacturing Investment Tax Credit to cover development expenses for brownfield critical mineral mine sites and expands the priority mineral list.
original_text: Modifying the Clean Technology Manufacturing Investment Tax Credit to include critical mineral mine development expenses for brownfield sites while expanding the list of priority critical minerals. This will accelerate how we get critical minerals from “rock to road” and will create good jobs.
parent_title: Expand Existing Tax Credits for Critical Minerals Exploration and Extraction

title: Leverage the Canada Growth Fund to Accelerate Critical Minerals Investment
description: Utilizes the Canada Growth Fund to provide funding and accelerate private investment specifically into Canadian critical minerals projects of national significance.
original_text: Invest in ourselves by providing funding for projects of national and economic significance and leveraging the Canada Growth Fund to accelerate private investment in Canadian critical minerals projects. This will deliver strong value for taxpayers and make Canada an even more attractive market for investments.

title: Build Thousands of New Electric Vehicle Charging Stations by 2027
description: Sets a target to expand the electric vehicle infrastructure network by building thousands of new charging stations by 2027.
original_text: Expand Canada’s electric vehicle (EV) charging network by supporting the building of thousands of new stations by 2027. We will also look at ways to reintroduce a purchase

title: Enhance workforce training and labour mobility
description: A broad commitment to improve workforce training, upskilling, and labour mobility across Canada to address labour shortages and prepare for future economic needs.
original_text: As we build a new economy for Canada, workers will need the skills for new jobs. Training is a fast track to higher wages and new opportunities.

title: Increase labour mobility for skilled trades workers across provinces
description: Focuses on increasing internal trade by removing barriers for skilled tradespeople to work across provincial and territorial borders, addressing regional labour shortages.
original_text: Increase labour mobility for skilled trades people between provinces and territories to address labour shortages. By increasing internal trade, we will build one Canadian economy – instead of thirteen – where qualified workers can work anywhere in Canada.
parent_title: Enhance workforce training and labour mobility

title: Expand the Labour Mobility Tax Deduction
description: Expands the tax deduction for workers traveling over 120 km to a job site and commits to increasing the annual tax deduction limit following industry consultations.
original_text: Expand the Labour Mobility Tax Deduction to ensure workers who travel more than 120 km from their home to a job site can deduct their expenses. We are also committed to significantly increasing the per-year tax deduction limit upon consultation with key industries.
parent_title: Enhance workforce training and labour mobility

title: Launch a $15,000 training and upskilling benefit for mid-career workers
description: Introduces a new financial benefit targeted at mid-career workers in high-priority sectors such as manufacturing, healthcare, construction, AI, and technology to support retention and upskilling.
original_text: Launch a new training and upskilling benefit worth up to $15,000 for workers in the middle of their careers. This benefit will be targetted to workers in priority sectors including manufacturing, health care, construction, AI, and technology.
parent_title: Enhance workforce training and labour mobility

title: Provide career support for workers impacted by tariffs
description: Partners with labour, employers, and industry to design tools and supports that help tariff-impacted workers transition to good-paying jobs in priority sectors.
original_text: Ensure that Canadian workers impacted by tariffs have the necessary tools to pursue good paying jobs and advance their careers in priority sectors. We will design these supports in partnership with labour, employers, and industry.
parent_title: Enhance workforce training and labour mobility

title: Align federal training programs with future labour market and AI needs
description: Tracks the labour market impacts of artificial intelligence to rapidly adjust federal training programs, focusing on building domestic capabilities in AI and cybersecurity.
original_text: Futureproof the workforce by ensuring federally funded training programs better align with labour market needs... We will track labour market impacts of AI to rapidly adjust training programs, so workers are continually ready for the jobs of the future.
parent_title: Enhance workforce training and labour mobility

title: Diversify trade and build new markets for Canadian goods
description: A comprehensive strategy to diversify Canadian trade away from reliance on the United States by seeking new markets, modernizing trade laws, and building trade infrastructure.
original_text: We need to diversify our supply chains so that we can have a more resilient, prosperous economy that isn’t dependent on any one country.
source_section: Building New Markets for Canadian Goods

title: Open a $25 billion export credit facility
description: Creates a massive credit facility to provide financing that helps foreign nations procure Canadian goods and services, facilitating international expansion for domestic businesses.
original_text: Open a $25 billion export credit facility to support Canadian businesses as they expand into new markets. This program offers financing to help other countries procure Canadian goods and services
source_section: Building New Markets for Canadian Goods
parent_title: Diversify trade and build new markets for Canadian goods

title: Expand and pivot the CanExport program
description: Refocuses the CanExport program to help businesses diversify away from the US market and maximize existing trade agreements like CETA and CPTPP.
original_text: Help Canadian businesses diversify their markets by expanding the CanExport program and pivoting it to focus on diversifying from the United States and fully take advantage of our existing trade deals, such as CETA and CPTPP.
source_section: Building New Markets for Canadian Goods
parent_title: Diversify trade and build new markets for Canadian goods

title: Pursue new trade deals with MERCOSUR and ASEAN
description: Aims to establish new free trade agreements with the MERCOSUR and ASEAN blocs to unlock access to markets valued at $9 trillion.
original_text: Pursue new trade deals with MERCOSUR and ASEAN so that Canadian businesses can access markets worth $9 trillion combined.
source_section: Building New Markets for Canadian Goods
parent_title: Diversify trade and build new markets for Canadian goods

title: Modernize the Special Import Measures Act
description: Updates trade remedy tools to combat circumvention or diversion, including legislating specific, express timelines for safeguard actions.
original_text: Modernize Canada’s Special Import Measures Act to respond to the new international trade reality, particularly to update Canada’s trade remedy tools in situations of circumvention or diversion, including legislating express timelines for safeguard actions.
source_section: Building New Markets for Canadian Goods
parent_title: Diversify trade and build new markets for Canadian goods

title: Build trade-enabling infrastructure
description: Invests in infrastructure projects specifically designed to support trade diversification away from the United States and strengthen the internal Canadian economy.
original_text: Build trade enabling infrastructure to diversify our trade away from the United States, create new jobs, and build one Canadian economy [more].
source_section: Building New Markets for Canadian Goods
parent_title: Diversify trade and build new markets for Canadian goods

title: Improve federal fiscal management and government efficiency
description: An overarching strategy to improve government efficiency, separate operational and capital spending, review expenditures, and balance the budget.
original_text: Efficiency and effectiveness must guide everything government does... It’s time for Canada to have a government focused on maximizing investments that drive growth and delivering results.

title: Balance the operating budget by Budget 2028
description: Commits to eliminating the gap between revenues and total operating spending, currently estimated at $15 billion annually, achieving a balanced operating budget by 2028.
original_text: Balance the operating budget by Budget 2028, ensuring responsible fiscal management while making wise, long-term investments to build for Canada’s prosperity and future.
parent_title: Improve federal fiscal management and government efficiency

title: Ensure federal capital investments catalyze private investment
description: Mandates that federal capital spending multiplies its value by attracting private sector investment, aiming to maximize job creation and boost worker incomes.
original_text: Ensure the federal government’s capital investment dollars catalyze multiple times their value in private investment. This will maximize job creation, boost incomes for Canadian workers
parent_title: Improve federal fiscal management and government efficiency

title: Ensure the government debt-to-GDP ratio declines
description: Pledges to maintain a downward trajectory for the debt-to-GDP ratio over the budget horizon through responsible fiscal management.
original_text: Ensure that government debt-to-GDP declines over the budget horizon.
parent_title: Improve federal fiscal management and government efficiency

title: Protect federal transfers to individuals, provinces, and territories
description: Guarantees the protection of key financial transfers including pensions, EI, CCB, Equalization, the Canada Health Transfer, and the Canada Social Transfer.
original_text: Protect transfers to individuals (e.g. pensions, EI, CCB payments, etc.) and to provinces and territories (e.g. Equalization, Canadian Health Transfer, Canada Social Transfer, and Territorial Formula Financing).
parent_title: Improve federal fiscal management and government efficiency

title: Protect key social programs including child care, dental care, and pharmacare
description: Commits to maintaining funding for major social initiatives like $10-a-day child care, the federal dental care program, and pharmacare despite other fiscal tightening.
original_text: Protect social programs including $10-a-day child care, dental care, and pharmacare [more].
parent_title: Improve federal fiscal management and government efficiency

title: Legislate the separation of capital and operating spending
description: Introduces legislation to formally separate capital and operating expenses in government accounting, supported by granting new powers and resources to the Parliamentary Budget Office.
original_text: To do that we will separate capital and operating spending within the government, and back this with a change in legislation that will be supported by new powers and resources for the Parliamentary Budget Office.
parent_title: Improve federal fiscal management and government efficiency

title: Launch a comprehensive review of government spending
description: Initiates a productivity-focused spending review across departments and Crown Corporations, aiming to amalgamate services, consolidate grants, and reduce reliance on external consultants.
original_text: A Mark Carney-led government will launch a comprehensive review of government spending in order to increase the federal government’s productivity. This review will focus on clear targets by departments and Crown Corporations with an iterative process that deploys best approaches across the public sector.
parent_title: Improve federal fiscal management and government efficiency

title: Cap federal public service employment
description: Commits to capping rather than cutting the size of the federal public service, ensuring staffing levels align with the needs of Canadians while controlling operational costs.
original_text: We are also committed to capping, not cutting, public service employment. Federal workers deliver essential services to Canadians and are critical to helping Canada meet this moment of crisis.
parent_title: Improve federal fiscal management and government efficiency

title: Enhance tax fairness and strengthen enforcement
description: A group of commitments focused on reforming the corporate tax system, advancing international tax rules, and leveraging technology to combat tax evasion.
original_text: Having a fair tax system is how we make sure we have resources needed to support Canadians, protect institutions like public health care, and ensure we can make the critical investments needed to make Canada strong.

title: Conduct an expert review of the corporate tax system
description: Commissions a comprehensive review of corporate taxation focused on improving fairness, transparency, simplicity, sustainability, and global competitiveness.
original_text: Conduct an expert review of the corporate tax system based on the principles of fairness, transparency, simplicity, sustainability, and competitiveness.
parent_title: Enhance tax fairness and strengthen enforcement

title: Lead G7 efforts to implement fair international tax rules
description: Commits to leading international negotiations with European and G7 partners to finalize and enforce the OECD's proposed framework for international corporate taxation.
original_text: Lead an international effort with partners in Europe and across the G7 to get a fair and consistent set of international tax rules completed, as proposed by the Organization for Economic Cooperation and Development (OECD).
parent_title: Enhance tax fairness and strengthen enforcement

title: Leverage technology at the CRA to prosecute tax evasion
description: Deploys advanced technology at the Canada Revenue Agency to identify tax evasion, close loopholes, and enhance overall enforcement capabilities.
original_text: Leverage technology at the Canada Revenue Agency to better identify and prosecute instances of tax evasion, fix loopholes, and strengthen enforcement.
parent_title: Enhance tax fairness and strengthen enforcement

title: Rebuild and modernize the Canadian Armed Forces
description: A comprehensive strategy to rebuild, rearm, and reinvest in the Canadian Armed Forces through significant funding increases and procurement modernization.
original_text: The single largest individual investment in this plan relates to rebuilding, rearming, and reinvesting in the Canadian Armed Forces.

title: Add $30.9 billion to defence spending over four years
description: Commits a massive $30.9 billion cash infusion into the military over four years, with half of the new investment in Year 4 dedicated specifically to purchasing new equipment.
original_text: More than $30.9 billion will be added to defence-related spending through this plan over the next four years, on a cash basis. As part of our plan, we intend to rapidly ramp up the portion of spending dedicated to new equipment and infrastructure such that by Year 4 half of this new investment is used to buy new kit for our men and women in uniform.
parent_title: Rebuild and modernize the Canadian Armed Forces

title: Exceed the NATO commitment to spend 2% of GDP on defence
description: Pledges to surpass the NATO target of spending at least 2% of Canada's Gross Domestic Product on defence and security.
original_text: With these investments we will exceed our commitment to spend at least 2% of GDP on defence.
parent_title: Rebuild and modernize the Canadian Armed Forces

title: Modernize defence procurement processes
description: Overhauls the military procurement system to ensure the Canadian Armed Forces can acquire necessary tools and equipment in a timely manner alongside trusted international partners.
original_text: Additionally, recognizing that it’s time to change the way Canada does procurement, we will modernize our defence procurement to ensure our Forces can buy the tools and equipment they need, in a timely way, working with trusted partners.
parent_title: Rebuild and modernize the Canadian Armed Forces

title: Integrate the Canadian Coast Guard into NATO defence capabilities
description: Plans to integrate the Coast Guard's operations into Canada's broader NATO defence framework, counting its funding toward the country's international defence spending commitments.
original_text: This does not include the additional funding that will count toward Canada’s NATO commitments as part of our plan to integrate the Canadian Coast Guard into our NATO defence capabilities.
parent_title: Rebuild and modernize the Canadian Armed Forces

title: Temporarily increase the Guaranteed Income Supplement by $652 per year
description: Seniors will receive a temporary, tax-free increase of $652 annually to their Guaranteed Income Supplement to support low-income older adults, particularly women.
original_text: Temporarily increasing the Guaranteed Income Supplement, giving seniors an additional $652 per year, tax-free.

title: Establish a permanent Youth Mental Health Fund
description: The government will create a permanent fund dedicated to supporting the mental health needs of young Canadians.
original_text: Establishing a permanent Youth Mental Health Fund, to help young Canadians.

title: Make $10-a-day child care permanent
description: Funding and agreements for $10-a-day child care will be made permanent to support families and boost women's workforce participation.
original_text: Making permanent $10/day child care and the National School Food Program, which will help children and families, including raising women’s participation rates in paid work.

title: Make the National School Food Program permanent
description: Funding for the National School Food Program will be made permanent to support families and children.
original_text: Making permanent $10/day child care and the National School Food Program, which will help children and families, including raising women’s participation rates in paid work.

title: Invest in clean technology and retrofits with provinces and territories
description: The government will fund and collaborate with provincial and territorial governments to accelerate the adoption of clean technologies and building retrofits.
original_text: Investing in clean growth and climate initiatives, such as working with provinces and territories to support the adoption of clean technology and retrofits

title: Eliminate the GST for first-time homebuyers
description: The Goods and Services Tax (GST) will be eliminated for first-time homebuyers to improve housing affordability for young Canadians.
original_text: Cutting the GST for First Time Homebuyers, which will help young Canadians.

title: Build high-speed rail in the Quebec-Windsor corridor
description: The government will invest in the construction of a high-speed rail network connecting major cities along the Quebec City-Windsor corridor.
original_text: Building high-speed rail to connect the Quebec-Windsor corridor

title: Invest in the Rural Transit Solutions Fund
description: Additional investments will be made into the Rural Transit Solutions Fund to improve public transportation access in rural communities.
original_text: investing in the Rural Transit Solutions Fund.

title: Invest in women's sexual and reproductive health care and research
description: Funding will be directed toward sexual and reproductive health care services and research, with specific inclusion of the 2SLGBTQI+ community.
original_text: Investing in women’s sexual and reproductive health care and health research, including for those within the 2SLGBTQI+ community.

title: Provide the Health Care Workers Hero Tax Credit for Personal Support Workers
description: Personal Support Workers will be eligible to receive up to $1,100 annually via a new or expanded Health Care Workers Hero Tax Credit.
original_text: Providing Personal Support Workers (PSWs) with up to $1,100 a year through the Health Care Workers Hero Tax Credit.

title: Prioritize Indigenous youth access to post-secondary education and skills training
description: The government will enhance outreach efforts, specifically for the Apprenticeship Grant, to increase post-secondary and skills training participation among Indigenous youth.
original_text: Prioritizing access to post-secondary education and skills training for Indigenous youth, including through targeted outreach for the Apprenticeship Grant.

title: Make the 2SLGBTQI+ Community Capacity Fund permanent
description: Ongoing, permanent funding will be established for the 2SLGBTQI+ Community Capacity Fund to support advocacy and community organizations.
original_text: Making funding permanent for the 2SLGBTQI+ Community Capacity Fund.

title: Expand the Court Challenges Program
description: The Court Challenges Program will be expanded to provide greater financial support for Canadians seeking to affirm their Charter rights in court.
original_text: Enabling Canadians to take more action to affirm their rights by expanding the Court Challenges Program.
parent_title: Uphold the Charter and Advance Equal Rights

title: Increase funding for the LGBTQI+ International Assistance Program
description: The government will boost financial resources for the LGBTQI+ International Assistance Program to support global human rights initiatives.
original_text: Adding additional funding for the LGBTQI+ International Assistance Program.

title: Develop a Disability Justice Framework
description: A new Disability Justice Framework will be created to ensure people with disabilities have equal and unhindered access to the justice system.
original_text: Guaranteeing equal access to justice for people with disabilities by developing a Disability Justice Framework.
parent_title: Uphold the Charter and Advance Equal Rights

title: Reform the Disability Tax Credit application process
description: The government will conduct a review and implement reforms to simplify and improve the application process for the Disability Tax Credit.
original_text: Reviewing and reforming the process to apply for the Disability Tax Credit.
